headline: AI Chip Demand Fuels Record TSMC Revenue; Fed Signals Rate Hike Pause
summary: Global markets reacted to strong Q2 earnings from key AI chip players and cooling inflation data. TSMC and ASE Technology reported record revenues driven by AI demand, while Aehr Test Systems provided strong FY27 guidance. The Fed indicated a potential pause in rate hikes as PPI data eased, though geopolitical tensions around Iran's oil blockade persist.

bullets.lead: TSMC and ASE Technology reported record Q2 revenues driven by robust AI chip demand.

segments.text: TSMC's Q2 revenue reached NT$1.27 trillion (US$40.07B), up 36% YoY, at the top end of guidance due to strong AI chip production demand.
ASE Technology also saw record Q2 revenue of NT$191.06 billion (US$5.95B), up 26.7% YoY, primarily from AI-related sales.

bullets.lead: Aehr Test Systems provided strong FY27 revenue guidance, signaling continued demand for burn-in solutions.

segments.text: Aehr Test Systems projected FY27 revenue of $130M-$150M, significantly exceeding estimates, supported by record backlog and demand for its burn-in solutions.
Hyperscaler custom AI processor programs are advancing into multi-generation production, with a lead customer placing a $41M order for package-level test.

bullets.lead: ASML plans price increases for chipmaking equipment amid strong AI demand and expansion.

segments.text: ASML is planning price increases for its chipmaking equipment, despite resistance from major clients like TSMC.

sources.title: ASML Plans Price Increases for Chipmaking Equipment, Despite TSMC Resistance
Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: BlackRock, PayPal, ASML, Morgan Stanley & more

segments.text: The company's Q2 revenue reached 9.3 billion euros, exceeding expectations, with plans to expand EUV equipment production by 30% next year to meet AI demand.

sources.title: AI需求太強！ASML第二季營收93億歐元、獲利優預期 EUV設備明年擬擴產30％

bullets.lead: Samsung is expanding DRAM production, while Nvidia addresses H200 shipments and Rubin Ultra rumors.

segments.text: Samsung Electronics plans to build a new DRAM fab at its Giheung campus with a monthly capacity of 100,000 wafers to meet surging AI-driven memory demand.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang debunked rumors of Rubin Ultra delays, confirming it is on track for next year, while limited H200 AI chip shipments have gone to China and Hong Kong despite license approvals.

bullets.lead: Cooling PPI data and Fed comments suggest a potential easing of rate hike pressures.

segments.text: The dollar slipped for a second consecutive day as cooling Producer Price Index (PPI) data eased rate hike expectations.

sources.title: Dollar slips for a second straight day as cooling PPI data eases rate hike bets

segments.text: Economic activity is rising and inflation is easing slightly, according to a recent Fed survey.

sources.title: Economic activity rising and inflation easing slightly, Fed survey shows

segments.text: Fed Governor Lisa Cook stated she is prepared to act soon if inflation does not begin to slow.

sources.title: Fed's Cook says she is prepared to act soon if inflation does not begin to slow

bullets.lead: Gold prices remained stable despite positive PPI data and escalating geopolitical tensions.

segments.text: Gold saw little change as positive PPI inflation data was offset by the U.S.-Iran escalation.

sources.title: Gold little changed as positive PPI inflation data offset by U.S.-Iran escalation

bullets.lead: South Korea's central bank is expected to raise its benchmark interest rate.

segments.text: The Bank of Korea is likely to raise its benchmark interest rate, marking the first tightening in 3 years and 6 months.

sources.title: 한은, 기준금리 인상 유력…3년 6개월 만에 긴축 돌입

bullets.lead: The U.S. reinstated an Iran blockade, causing oil prices to tick higher and raising market concerns.

segments.text: Oil prices ticked higher after the U.S. reinstated an Iran blockade, with traders warning the market is close to running on empty as the Strait of Hormuz faces potential disruptions.

sources.title: What Trump’s New Iran Blockade Could Mean for Oil Prices
Oil traders warn market is close to running on empty as Hormuz shuts again
Saudi Gulf Oil Loads Slump as Iran Hits Tankers in Hormuz

bullets.lead: China's economy is growing at its slowest pace in years, while Apple secured approval for Alibaba-powered AI tools.

segments.text: China's economy is experiencing its slowest growth in years, reflecting ongoing challenges.

sources.title: China’s Economy Grows at Slowest Pace in Years

segments.text: Apple has received approval to integrate Alibaba-powered iPhone AI tools in China, potentially expanding its AI offerings in the region.

sources.title: Apple Gets Approval for Alibaba-Powered iPhone AI Tools in China

bullets.lead: Chinese semiconductor firms are undergoing significant restructuring and leadership changes.

segments.text: Changxin Memory Technologies (CXMT) founder, with 7 years of unpaid management, could see assets up to 20 trillion won after the company's listing.

sources.title: [중국 특징주] 7년 무보수 경영 'CXMT' 창업자, 상장 후 자산 최대 20조 ...

segments.text: Zhengzhou Semiconductor Group is undergoing major asset restructuring to expand its semiconductor investments.

sources.title: [중국 특징주] 정측전자그룹, 중대 자산 재편 '반도체 투자 확대'

bullets.lead: Wall Street closed higher, buoyed by strong earnings and favorable inflation data.

segments.text: U.S. markets ended higher, supported by cool inflation data and robust earnings reports.

sources.title: Wall St ends higher on cool inflation data, strong earnings

bullets.lead: Morgan Stanley reported record quarterly revenue and profit, driven by a surge in equities trading.

segments.text: Morgan Stanley posted record quarterly revenue and profit, with equities trading surging by 69%.

sources.title: Morgan Stanley posts record quarterly revenue and profit as equities trading surges 69%

bullets.lead: PSMC increased DRAM foundry prices, targeting a significant revenue share from 3D AI Foundry.

segments.text: PSMC lifted DRAM foundry prices by 45%, with its 3D AI Foundry business aiming for a 20% revenue share.

sources.title: PSMC lifts DRAM foundry prices 45% as 3D AI Foundry targets 20% revenue share

bullets.lead: DeepSeek's annualized revenue is nearing $500 million, boosting its fundraise and IPO plans.

segments.text: DeepSeek's annualized revenue is approaching $500 million, which is expected to support its fundraising efforts and potential IPO.

sources.title: DeepSeek’s Annualized Revenue Nears $500 Million, Boosting Fundraise and IPO Plans

bullets.lead: Microsoft is overhauling its security leadership and providing sales tips against AI competitors.

segments.text: Microsoft's new security chief is replacing top executives to force an AI overhaul within the company.

sources.title: Microsoft’s New Security Chief Replaces Top Execs to Force an AI Overhaul

segments.text: Microsoft is also providing its salespeople with tips to counter competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.

sources.title: Microsoft Gives Salespeople Tips to Knock Down Anthropic, OpenAI

bullets.lead: OpenAI is reportedly planning to unveil a home speaker as its first hardware product.

segments.text: OpenAI is reportedly developing a home speaker, which would be its first hardware product, featuring a camera, sensors, and advanced AI models for contextual understanding.

bullets.lead: Amazon AWS is seeing executive changes, while Palantir's role in government and AI warnings draw scrutiny.

segments.text: Dave Brown, an Amazon AWS executive and CEO advisor, is set to leave the company after 19 years.

sources.title: Amazon AWS executive and CEO advisor Dave Brown to leave after 19 years

segments.text: Concerns have been raised about Palantir's growing foothold in the British state, with its AI capabilities drawing warnings from Microsoft's CEO.

sources.title: I investigated Palantir’s foothold in the British state – and what I found should worry us all | Peter Geoghegan
Microsoft CEO adds fuel to Palantir CEO’s AI warning

watchlist.name: Samsung Electronics

x_themes.topic: Aehr Test Systems FY27 revenue guidance
x_themes.summary: Aehr Test Systems provided strong FY27 revenue guidance of $130M-$150M, significantly exceeding estimates, driven by record backlog and demand for its burn-in solutions.

x_themes.topic: Innolight reports increased 800G demand and broad supply chain shortages
x_themes.summary: Innolight's investor relations takeaways indicate significantly increased 800G demand and broad supply chain shortages across optical/electrical chips, PCBs, and other module materials, with silicon photonics penetration continuing to trend upward.

x_themes.topic: AAOI Texas manufacturing expansion for optical transceivers
x_themes.summary: AAOI announced the commencement of its Texas manufacturing expansion, adding nearly 400,000 sq ft for 800G/1.6T optical transceiver production, targeting significant capacity increases by mid-2027.

x_themes.topic: Hyperscaler Custom AI ASICs entering multi-generation production
x_themes.summary: Aehr's earnings call indicates hyperscaler custom AI processor programs are progressing into multi-generation production, with a lead customer placing a $41M order for package-level burn-in systems for their 1st, 2nd (2x power), and 3rd gen devices.

x_themes.topic: TSMC Q2 revenue reaches record high on AI chip demand
x_themes.summary: TSMC's Q2 revenue reached a record NT$1.27 trillion (US$40.07B), up 36% YoY and at the top end of guidance, driven by strong demand for AI chip production.

x_themes.topic: ASE Technology Q2 revenue record high on AI sales
x_themes.summary: ASE Technology's Q2 revenue reached a record NT$191.06 billion (US$5.95B), up 26.7% YoY, driven by strong AI-related sales.

x_themes.topic: Pre-package screening becoming more valuable for advanced packaging
x_themes.summary: Rising economic value in advanced packages makes pre-package reliability screening, like wafer-level burn-in, increasingly valuable to improve effective advanced-packaging yield and reduce waste.

x_themes.topic: Wafer-level burn-in displacing package/system-level test spending
x_themes.summary: Wafer-level burn-in (WLBI) is gaining traction, with one customer eliminating system-level screening and another considering moving from package-level to wafer-level burn-in, potentially displacing traditional post-package test equipment.

x_themes.topic: Increased infrastructure content per AI accelerator due to higher power
x_themes.summary: Next-generation AI silicon, with 2x package power, is driving higher infrastructure requirements for data centers, benefiting suppliers of power distribution, thermal management, and liquid cooling equipment.

OpenAI reportedly plans to unveil a home speaker as its first hardware product, featuring a camera, sensors, and advanced AI models for understanding surroundings and context, with a 2027 release target.

x_themes.topic: Limited Nvidia H200 shipments to China/Hong Kong
x_themes.summary: Very few Nvidia H200 AI chips have shipped to China and Hong Kong despite license approvals, indicating a restart under new arrangements but with strict U.S. government review and compliance inspections.

x_themes.topic: Silicon Carbide (SiC) wafer prices rising due to AI/800V EV demand
x_themes.summary: Silicon Carbide (SiC) wafer prices are increasing due to rising demand for power semiconductors in AI data centers and 800V DC electric vehicles, leading to tight supplies and premium pricing for additional wafers.

x_themes.topic: Nvidia debunks Rubin Ultra delay rumors and addresses competition
x_themes.summary: Jensen Huang debunked rumors of Rubin Ultra delays, confirming it's on track for next year, and addressed ASIC competition by noting a frontier lab shifted 50% of its compute to Nvidia.

x_themes.topic: Samsung Electronics building new DRAM fab at Giheung
x_themes.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to build a new DRAM fab at its Giheung campus with a monthly capacity of 100,000 wafers, converting a former R&D site to meet surging AI-driven memory demand.
